item.title: 🤝 Merger · USAR
item.ns0:headline: Files supplemental disclosures for pending SVRE merger
item.description:
Files supplemental disclosures for pending SVRE merger

Filed supplemental disclosures, audited financials of SVRE, and pro forma combined statements for its pending acquisition of SVRE Holdings Ltd. via merger. The merger was announced on April 19, 2026; the filing updates risk factors and provides financial information. No per-share consideration or deal value was disclosed in this filing.

About: USA Rare Earth, Inc. operates as a magnet manufacturing company. The Company develops a NdFeB magnet manufacturing, minerals supply, extraction, and processing plant. USA Rare Earth serves defense, automotive, aviation, industrial, medical, and consumer electronics industries in the United States.

  • Counterparty: SVRE Holdings Ltd.
  • Announced: 2026-04-19

item.ns0:headline: Issues 126.8M shares to acquire SVRE Holdings; vote at special meeting
item.description:
Issues 126.8M shares to acquire SVRE Holdings; vote at special meeting

Will acquire SVRE Holdings Ltd. in a stock-for-stock merger, issuing 126,849,307 shares of common stock to SVRE securityholders. The merger requires stockholder approval at a special meeting; closing is expected by the end of Q3 2026. The board unanimously recommends voting FOR the share issuance proposal.

item.title: 💧 Capital Return · Buyback · ACVA
item.ns0:headline: Accelerated share repurchase of $50M with Citibank; initial delivery ~70% of shares
item.description:
Accelerated share repurchase of $50M with Citibank; initial delivery ~70% of shares

Entered an accelerated share repurchase agreement with Citibank to repurchase $50 million of its common stock. The company will make an initial payment of $50 million on May 13, 2026, and expects to receive approximately 70% of the shares upfront. The final number of shares will be based on VWAP over the term, with completion expected by Q4 2026.

About: ACV Auctions Inc. operates a digital marketplace that connects buyers and sellers for the online auction of wholesale vehicles. It also provides data services that offer insights into the condition and value of used vehicles, as well as offers customer financing services. ACV Auctions Inc. was incorporated in 2014 and is headquartered in Buffalo, New York.

  • Counterparty: Citibank, N.A.
  • Deal value: $50,000,000
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-12
  • Expected Close: Q4 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · ASRT
item.ns0:headline: Acquired by Zydus for $23.50/share cash; tender offer to commence; outside date July 12
item.description:
Acquired by Zydus for $23.50/share cash; tender offer to commence; outside date July 12

Entered into a definitive merger agreement with Zydus Worldwide DMCC, under which Zydus will acquire all outstanding shares via a cash tender offer at $23.50 per share, followed by a back-end merger. The tender offer is subject to minimum tender of >50% of shares and Assertio having at least $95M in closing net cash; no financing condition. The deal includes a $6.26M termination fee and is expected to close by July 12, 2026.

About: Assertio Holdings, Inc., a specialty pharmaceutical company, provides medicines in the areas of neurology, hospital, and pain and inflammation. Its pharmaceutical products include INDOCIN, an oral solution and a suppository form for the treatment of moderate to severe rheumatoid arthritis, including acute flares of chronic disease; ankylosing spondylitis and osteoarthritis; and acute painful shoulder and gouty arthritis.

  • Counterparty: Zydus Worldwide DMCC
  • Offer price: 23.50
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-13
  • Expected Close: July 12, 2026 (outside date)

Received notice from Nasdaq Hearings Panel that its common stock will be delisted from Nasdaq due to failure to maintain minimum $1.00 bid price. Suspension begins May 14, 2026; stock will trade on OTCQB under symbol RVPH.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · MBRX
item.ns0:headline: Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical
item.description:
Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical

Announced imminent unblinding of first 45 subjects from its pivotal Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial evaluating Annamycin + cytarabine in relapsed/refractory AML. Preliminary blinded data show a complete remission rate of approximately 30%, compared to historical 17-18% for cytarabine alone. Unblinding is expected before June 30, 2026, with Part A recruitment on track to reach 90 subjects in Q3 2026.

About: Moleculin Biotech, Inc., a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company, focuses on the development of drug candidates for the treatment of highly resistant tumors and viruses. Its lead drug candidate is Annamycin that is in Phase 1/2 studies for the treatment of relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and cancers metastasized to the lungs.

  • Announced: 2026-05-13

Mkt cap $2.8M · EV $30.7M · EV/Sales 0.4x · Healthcare / Biotechnology

item.title: 🤝 Merger · TWO
item.ns0:headline: Rejects UWMC's $12.50/share bid; reaffirms CCM $12.00 cash merger vote May 19
item.description:
Rejects UWMC's $12.50/share bid; reaffirms CCM $12.00 cash merger vote May 19

Board unanimously rejected the revised unsolicited proposal from UWM Holdings (UWMC) of $12.50 per share (default UWMC stock worth $7.58), determining it does not constitute a superior proposal. The board continues to recommend the pending all-cash merger with CrossCountry Mortgage at $12.00 per share, representing a 21% premium to the unaffected share price. A special meeting of stockholders is scheduled for May 19, 2026, and 35 of 53 required regulatory approvals have been received.

About: Two Harbors Investment Corp. operates as a real estate investment trust (REIT) that focuses on investing in, financing, and managing residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS), non-agency securities, mortgage servicing rights, and other financial assets in the United States. Its target assets include agency RMBS collateralized by fixed rate mortgage loans, adjustable rate mortgage loans, and hybrid adjustable-rate mortgage (ARMs); and other assets, such as financial and mortgage-related asset…

  • Counterparty: CrossCountry Mortgage, LLC
  • Offer price: $12.00 per share
  • Premium: 21%
  • Consideration: cash

item.title: 🎯 Tender · KALV
item.ns0:headline: Chiesi launches $27.00/share cash tender; expires June 10
item.description:
Chiesi launches $27.00/share cash tender; expires June 10

Chiesi Farmaceutici, through its subsidiary Skyline Merger Sub, commenced a cash tender offer for all outstanding shares of KalVista Pharmaceuticals at $27.00 per share. The offer expires at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on June 10, 2026, unless extended. The board unanimously recommends tendering; the subsequent merger under Section 251(h) of the DGCL will not require a stockholder vote.

About: KalVista Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical stage pharmaceutical company, discovers, develops, and commercializes small molecule protease inhibitors for diseases with unmet needs. The company's product portfolio comprises small molecule plasma kallikrein inhibitors targeting hereditary angioedema (HAE) and diabetic macular edema (DME); and oral plasma kallikrein inhibitors.

  • Counterparty: Chiesi Farmaceutici S.p.A.

  • Deal value: $1.44 billion
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-04-29

item.title: 🧭 Domicile · XPRO
item.ns0:headline: Reincorporation via two-step cross-border merger; vote June 10
item.description:
Reincorporation via two-step cross-border merger; vote June 10

Expro proposes a two-step downstream cross-border merger: first into Expro Luxembourg S.A., then into Expro Ltd, effectively changing its corporate domicile. Shareholders will vote on the transaction at the AGM on June 10, 2026. The filing also includes amendments to articles of association to govern withdrawal rights.

About: Expro Group Holdings N.V. engages in the provision of energy services in North and Latin America, Europe and Sub-Saharan Africa, the Middle East and North Africa, and the Asia-Pacific. The company provides well construction services, such as technology solutions in drilling, tubular running services, and cementing and tubulars; and well management services, including well flow management, subsea well access, and well intervention and integrity services.

  • Expected Close: Q2 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · EQX
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Orla Mining in all-stock merger; 1.00x exchange ratio
item.description:
Acquires Orla Mining in all-stock merger; 1.00x exchange ratio

Acquires Orla Mining in an all-stock transaction. Orla shareholders receive 1.00 Equinox share per Orla share, with existing Equinox holders owning 67% and Orla holders 33% of the combined company. The deal is subject to shareholder and court approvals; the combined entity expects 1.1M oz annual gold production with a path to 1.9M oz.

About: Equinox Gold Corp. engages in the operation, acquisition, exploration, and development of mineral properties. The company primarily explores for gold and silver deposits. Its properties include the Aurizona gold mine located in Maranhão State; the RDM gold mine located in Minas Gerais State; and Fazenda gold mine and the Santa Luz gold mine located in Bahia State, Brazil.

  • Counterparty: Orla Mining Ltd.
  • Consideration: stock

item.title: 📈 Rights · EOSE
item.ns0:headline: Rights offering up to $150M to fund Cerberus JV; shareholder vote pending
item.description:
Rights offering up to $150M to fund Cerberus JV; shareholder vote pending

Entered a binding term sheet with Cerberus affiliate CCM Frontier to form a JV. Cerberus will contribute $100M for Class A-2 units and receive warrants exercisable at a 20% discount to 15-day VWAP. Eos will raise up to $150M via a rights offering to fund its Class B contribution. Closing requires DOE consent, shareholder approval of authorized share increase, and execution of commercial framework.

About: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. designs, manufactures, and deploys battery storage solutions for utility, commercial and industrial, and renewable energy markets in the United States. It offers stationary battery storage solutions. The company's flagship product is the Eos Znyth DC battery system designed to meet the requirements of the grid-scale energy storage market. Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. was founded in 2008 and is headquartered in Edison, New Jersey.

  • Counterparty: Cerberus Capital Management (CCM Frontier JV Holdco, LLC)
  • Deal value: $150,000,000
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-12

item.title: 🤝 Merger · ORLA
item.ns0:headline: Merges with Equinox Gold in all-stock deal; 1.00 EQX per ORLA share
item.description:
Merges with Equinox Gold in all-stock deal; 1.00 EQX per ORLA share

Will be acquired by Equinox Gold in an at-market stock-for-stock merger. Orla shareholders will receive 1.00 Equinox common share (plus $0.0001 cash) per Orla share, representing approximately 33% ownership of the combined company. The combined entity, named Equinox Gold, is expected to produce 1.1 million ounces of gold annually with a clear path to 1.9 million ounces. The transaction is subject to shareholder and court approvals.

About: Orla Mining Ltd. acquires, explores for, and develops mineral properties. The company explores for gold, silver, zinc, lead, and copper deposits. It owns 100% interests in the Camino Rojo project that consists of seven concessions covering an area of 163,129 hectares located in Zacatecas, Mexico; and Cerro Quema project totaling an area of 14,800 hectares located in the Azuero Peninsula, Panama. The company was formerly known as Red Mile Minerals Corp. and changed its name to Orla Mining Ltd.

  • Counterparty: Equinox Gold Corp.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-13

Mkt cap $5.00B · EV $4.38B · Fwd P/E 7.8x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 12.9x · EV/Sales 2.5x · Basic Materials / Gold

item.title: 💧 Capital Return · Self Tender · EXFY
item.ns0:headline: Commences $25M Dutch auction tender at $0.98-$1.20/share; expires June 10
item.description:
Commences $25M Dutch auction tender at $0.98-$1.20/share; expires June 10

Commenced a modified Dutch auction tender offer to repurchase up to $25M of its Class A common stock at a price between $0.98 and $1.20 per share. The offer is not conditioned on a minimum number of shares or financing, and will expire at midnight on June 10, 2026. Directors and executive officers will not tender any shares.

About: Expensify, Inc. provides a cloud-based expense management software platform to individuals, small businesses, and corporations in the United States and internationally. The company's platform enables users to manage corporate cards, pay bills, generate invoices, collect payments, and book travel. It also offers track and submit plans for individuals. The company was founded in 2008 and is based in Portland, Oregon.

The company was founded in 2008 and is based in Portland, Oregon.

item.ns0:headline: Enters fifth forbearance with J.J. Astor; $1.5M new convertible note, 1M shares issued
item.description:
Enters fifth forbearance with J.J. Astor; $1.5M new convertible note, 1M shares issued

Entered a Fifth Forbearance Agreement with lender J.J. Astor & Co., modifying repayment terms on the outstanding Second Note ($5,995,722). The agreement includes a new $1,500,000 convertible note (the Fifth Note) with proceeds of $1,050,000, and issuance of 1,000,000 commitment shares. Repayment schedule extended to January 1, 2027, with weekly installments starting at $50,000 and increasing to $250,000. The company remains in default but avoids acceleration.

About: Vivakor, Inc. operates, acquires, and develops clean energy technologies and environmental solutions primarily focused on soil remediation in the United States and Kuwait. It specializes in the remediation of soil and the extraction of hydrocarbons, such as oil from properties contaminated by or laden with heavy crude oil and other hydrocarbon-based substances. The company was founded in 2006 and is based in Lehi, Utah.

The company has 180 calendar days, until November 4, 2026, to regain compliance by achieving a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for ten consecutive business days. If not cured, the company may be eligible for an additional 180-day period or face delisting.

item.ns0:headline: Sells Transportation & Vendor Finance businesses to Stonepeak for cash + earnout; retains 19.9% stake
item.description:
Sells Transportation & Vendor Finance businesses to Stonepeak for cash + earnout; retains 19.9% stake

BMO signed a definitive agreement to sell its Transportation Finance and Vendor Finance businesses (combined loan/lease portfolio of ~C$14.5B) to Stonepeak for cash consideration plus an earnout. BMO will retain a 19.9% equity interest in the new entity. The transaction is expected to close in Q4 fiscal 2026, subject to regulatory approvals, and is expected to improve BMO's CET1 ratio by ~28 bps.

item.description:
Nasdaq staff determination April 28; seeks extension to June 1

Received a Nasdaq staff determination letter on April 28, 2026, for non-compliance with the $35M market value of listed securities rule (MVLS Rule). The company completed a merger on May 1, 2026, issuing $20M Series B Preferred to address equity compliance, and stockholders approved a reverse stock split (1:3 to 1:30) on May 8, 2026, to regain bid price compliance. The Nasdaq Hearings Panel previously granted an exception through June 1, 2026, for bid price compliance, and the company is requesting an extension.

It primarily invests in dividend paying stocks of companies.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Fda Pre Bla Meeting Positive · MLTX
item.ns0:headline: FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027
item.description:
FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027

Announced positive outcome from final pre-BLA meeting with FDA for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). FDA aligned on submission plans and label strategy, including acceptability of MIRA trial data for effectiveness and inclusion of adolescent data from VELA-TEEN. BLA submission planned for end of September 2026; acceptance and Priority Review decision expected by end of November 2026; first commercial launch targeted for H2 2027 if approved. Company also reported Q1 2026 cash of $357.9M.

About: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, engages in developing therapies. It is developing Sonelokimab, a novel investigational Nanobody therapy for the treatment of inflammation. The company is involved in conducting Phase II trials for hidradenitis suppurativa, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, or radiographic axial spondyloarthritis. MoonLake Immunotherapeutics was founded in 2021 and is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland.

  • Announced: 2026-05-10

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · ADTX
item.ns0:headline: Forced Nasdaq delisting May 6; appeal hearing requested
item.description:
Forced Nasdaq delisting May 6; appeal hearing requested

Received a Staff Determination from Nasdaq on May 6, 2026, that its common stock will be delisted from The Nasdaq Capital Market due to the bid price closing below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days (March 24, 2026 through May 5, 2026). The company is ineligible for a 180-day compliance period because it has effected reverse stock splits over the prior two years with a cumulative ratio of 250 shares or more to one. Aditxt intends to timely request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the determination, which will stay delisting pending the hearing outcome.

About: Aditxt, Inc., a biotech company, develops technologies focuses on improving the health of the immune system through immune mapping and reprogramming. It develops AditxtScore that allows individuals to understand, manage, and monitor their immune profiles in order to be informed about attacks on or by their immune system; and Apoptotic DNA Immunotherapy, a nucleic acid-based technology that utilizes an approach that mimics the way the body naturally induces tolerance to its own tissues.

  • Announced: 2026-05-06

Al Shams Investments, the largest shareholder of Braemar Hotels & Resorts (BHR) with a 9.5% stake, announced its intention to seek election of new directors at the 2026 annual meeting. The activist criticizes the board's asset sale strategy, warning that further hotel divestitures could trigger a $480+ million termination payment to Ashford Inc., the external advisor controlled by Chairman Monty Bennett. Al Shams urges the independent directors to pause deal-making until shareholders elect a new board.

item.description:

Activist group BIMIZCI Fund LLC (led by Stephen Hodges of ZimCal Asset Management) filed a definitive proxy statement to elect its slate of three Class III director nominees at Medallion Financial Corp.'s 2026 annual meeting on June 9, 2026. BIMIZCI owns 407,000 shares (4th largest institutional holder) and plans to increase its stake, citing poor financial performance, an SEC lawsuit against CEO Andrew Murstein, and governance conflicts including a $3.4 million severance obligation tied to Alvin Murstein's nomination. The activist urges stockholders to vote FOR its nominees on the BLUE proxy card.

About: Medallion Financial Corp., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a finance company in the United States. The company operates through four segments: Recreation Lending, Home Improvement Lending, Commercial Lending, and Medallion Lending. It provides loans that finance consumer purchases of recreational vehicles, boats, and trailers; consumer home improvements; commercial businesses; and taxi medallions to individuals, and small to mid-size businesses.

TDS proposes to acquire the remaining 18.1% of Array Digital Infrastructure shares it does not already own via an all-stock merger, exchanging each Array share for 0.86 TDS shares. TDS currently holds 81.9% of Array's stock and 95.9% of voting interests. The proposal is non-binding and subject to negotiation of a definitive agreement, approval by a special committee of disinterested Array directors, disinterested Array stockholder approval, and TDS stockholder approval.

About: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc., a telecommunications company, provides communications services in the United States. It operates through two segments: UScellular and TDS Telecom. item.description:

Shareholders approved the business combination with Hadron Energy, Inc., including the issuance of up to 60 million shares of post-combination common stock to Hadron equity holders. The company intends to proceed with domestication from Cayman Islands to Delaware on May 8, 2026, and close the merger shortly thereafter. The vote passed with 19,681,540 shares for and 1,094,478 against.

item.title: 🔐 Going Private · AD
item.ns0:headline: TDS proposes on May 7 to acquire remaining 18.1% of AD; special committee formed
item.description:
TDS proposes on May 7 to acquire remaining 18.1% of AD; special committee formed

Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS), which already owns 81.9% of the outstanding stock and 95.9% of the voting interests, submitted a non-binding proposal on May 7, 2026 to acquire all remaining common shares of Array Digital Infrastructure. Array's board formed a special committee of independent directors, which retained PJT Partners as financial advisor and Cravath, Swaine & Moore as legal counsel, to evaluate the proposal. No price or definitive terms have been disclosed, and there is no assurance a transaction will occur.

About: Array Digital Infrastructure, Inc. provides wireless telecommunications services in the United States. The company offers wireless services, including voice, messaging, and data services. It also provides wireless devices, such as handsets, tablets, mobile hotspots, home phones, and routers, as well as wireless essentials, including cases, screen protectors, chargers, and memory cards; and consumer electronics comprising Bluetooth audio, wi-fi enabled cameras, and networking products.

  • Counterparty: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc.
  • Stake: 81.9
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-07

item.ns0:headline: Files for Singapore judicial management; hearing May 29; 3 directors resign
item.description:
Files for Singapore judicial management; hearing May 29; 3 directors resign

Filed voluntary applications for judicial management in Singapore on April 1, 2026, and was placed under interim judicial management on April 9, 2026. The hearing for the judicial management applications is scheduled for May 29, 2026. Three independent directors resigned in early May 2026.

About: Maxeon Solar Technologies, Ltd. designs, manufactures, markets, and sells solar panels and related solar system components worldwide. The company provides interdigitated back contact and shingled solar cells and panels under the SunPower brand. It offers its products to dealers, project developers, system integrators, distributors, resellers, and residential and small-scale commercial customers. Maxeon Solar Technologies, Ltd. was incorporated in 2019 and is headquartered in Singapore.
